app.title: Config Browser
app.settingsSubtitle: Manage app preferences, language, proxy, safety policy, and future advanced options.
app.auditPlanned: Planned for multi-environment consistency matrix, ignore rules, name normalization, and batch audits.
app.backupPlanned: Planned for cloud exports, local snapshots, and cloud-vs-local comparison.
app.tasksPlanned: Planned for long-running audit, backup, restore, and export jobs.
app.settingsPlanned: Planned for global proxy, safety policy, update checks, and advanced options.

settings.sshProfileNamePlaceholder: e.g. Company Bastion / Production Jump Host
settings.sshProfileRequired: Profile name, SSH server address, and username are required
settings.sshProfileDeleteBlocked: This SSH profile is referenced by {count} connections and cannot be deleted directly.
settings.sshProfileUpdateImpact: This SSH profile is referenced by {count} connections. Saving will make those connections use the new config immediately. Review the impact list and save again to confirm.

ssh.pageSubtitle: Manage reusable SSH jump-host profiles and references in one place. Connection Manager only selects or references these profiles.
ssh.profileListSubtitle: Reusable jump-host and bastion profiles across projects.
ssh.creatingProfile: Create once, then reuse it in Connection Manager.
ssh.basicInfoHint: Only enter SSH jump-host information, not the Nacos remote target.
ssh.authInfoHint: Credentials used to log in to the jump host. Sensitive handling will keep improving.
ssh.localPolicyHint: Usually leave the port auto-assigned to avoid cross-connection conflicts.
ssh.referencesHint: These project / environment / source entries are affected by this profile.
ssh.noReferences: This profile is not referenced by any connection yet.

connection.title: Connection Manager
connection.pageSubtitle: Organize config-center access by project, environment, and source. Source describes the target network or backup shape; SSH tunnel is configured separately as an access mode.
connection.name: Remark (optional)
connection.namePlaceholder: e.g. Company VPN only / Travel fallback / Read-only account
connection.nameHelp: Remark explains when to use this source config. It is not the sidebar title. The sidebar title uses Source Name; empty remark uses the source name internally.
connection.projectHelp: Project is the business-system level. Pick an existing project or type a new one.
connection.environmentHelp: Environment is a fixed runtime category under a project. Existing historical values stay in the dropdown so old connections keep their grouping.
connection.sourceNamePlaceholder: e.g. Cloud Public / Cloud Intranet / Company Intranet
connection.sourceNameHelp: One project environment can have multiple sources, such as cloud public, cloud intranet, company intranet, or travel network. SSH is an access mode, not a source category.
connection.sourcePresetHelp: Source entry describes which network or entry the target Nacos belongs to, such as cloud public, cloud intranet, or company intranet. Choose SSH tunnel separately in access mode.
connection.sourceTypeHelp: Online connection accesses Nacos directly. Local snapshot directory reads exported backup configs for comparison.
connection.localPathPlaceholder: Enter or choose a local folder path
connection.localPathHelp: Choose the exported config snapshot directory. Windows can use folder picker or type a path directly; Linux/macOS can type the path. The directory is validated after choosing.
connection.localPathStructureHint: Directory must contain confscope.snapshot.json / manifest.json / metadata.json / .metadata.yml, or configs/ / namespaces/ standard folders, and at least 1 comparable config file.
connection.forceLocalSnapshotHelp: Allow saving even when directory validation fails. Reading or comparison may fail later; use only when you know the directory is usable.
connection.localValidationRequired: Validate the local snapshot directory before saving
connection.localValidationOk: Directory is valid, found {count} config files
connection.defaultSourceHelp: When an environment has multiple sources, the default source is preferred by browse and compare entry points.
connection.addressHelp: Use the real Nacos URL, for example http://host:8848/nacos. With SSH tunnel, still enter the Nacos URL the tunnel should reach.
connection.usernameHelp: Use this for open-source Nacos username/password auth. Leave empty for no-auth.
connection.authTypeHelp: Choose the authentication mode used by the Nacos server. Aliyun MSE usually uses AccessKey.
connection.distributionHelp: Open-source Nacos uses username/password or no-auth. Aliyun MSE switches to AccessKey credentials.
connection.accessModeHelp: Access mode only describes the technical path. Even a cloud public source may need SSH tunnel or jump-host access because of firewall policy.
connection.securityTokenPlaceholder: Use this for temporary STS credentials
connection.securityTokenHelp: Only needed for temporary STS credentials. Long-term AccessKey usually leaves this empty.
connection.passwordHelp: Locally saved Nacos password. Encrypted storage will be improved later.
connection.accessKeyHelp: Aliyun MSE Nacos access credential. Use an AccessKey with config read permissions.
connection.nameAndAddressRequired: Source name and target address are required
connection.deleteNote: Local connection info will be removed, but won't affect the Nacos server.
connection.fieldsRequired: SSH address / port / username cannot be empty
connection.passwordRequired: Password auth: password cannot be empty
connection.keyRequired: Key auth: private key cannot be empty
connection.noConnections: No connections yet, create one on the right
connection.defaultNamespaceHelp: Nacos namespaceId. Empty means public; this is not the namespace display name.
connection.sshProfileHelp: Choose a reusable SSH jump-host profile. When a profile is selected, the connection stores only the reference instead of duplicating SSH credentials.
connection.sshProfileRequired: SSH server address and username are required
connection.sshHostHelp: Jump host or bastion address, not the Nacos address.
connection.sshPortHelp: SSH port of the jump host, usually 22.
connection.sshUsernameHelp: System user used to log in to the jump host.
connection.sshAuthHelp: Authentication mode used to log in to the jump host.
connection.sshPasswordHelp: SSH password for the jump host.
connection.localPortHelp: Temporary local listening port. Leave empty for automatic assignment in most cases.
connection.privateKeyHelp: Private key used to log in to the jump host.
connection.privateKeyPasswordHelp: Fill this only when the private key has a passphrase.

config.selectHint: Select a config from the left to view
config.discardMessage: Current config has unsaved edits. Switching will discard them.
config.deleteWarning: This action cannot be undone. The following config will be permanently deleted:
config.deleteHint: Please enter the dataId to confirm:

diff.title: Smart Config Compare
diff.selectHint: Select configs on both sides and click "Compare"
diff.noConnection: Please add Nacos connections in "Connection Manager" first
diff.supportHint:
Supports: Same Nacos two configs · Cross-namespace · Cross-environment comparison
Leave dataId empty to auto-match and batch compare same-name configs on both sides
diff.dataIdPlaceholder: Leave empty to match all same-name dataIds

history.title: History
history.rollbackNote: This will overwrite and publish to Nacos
history.pickHint: Pick 1 to compare with online · Pick 2 to compare each other
history.selectHint: Click a version on the left to view, or pick versions to compare

about.title: About
about.noUpdate: You are on the latest version
